title: lobelia pleotricha
intro: Lobelia pleotricha, a flowering plant native to the Andean region, belongs to the Campanulaceae family and is commonly known as 'Quechua Lobelia' or 'Andean Lobelia'. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits also include its potential in reducing stress, anxiety, and pain relief, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Lobelia pleotricha is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried leaves and flowers being used to create these preparations.
